summary: Security improvements with proper implementation. Code changes address critical security concerns.

issues.description: Consider adding a docstring comment explaining why 0o600 permissions are used
issues.suggestion: Add comment: # 0o600 = owner read/write only, prevents other users from accessing credentials

issues.description: os.chmod could raise OSError on Windows or if file doesn't exist
issues.suggestion: Wrap os.chmod in try-except block to handle platform-specific issues

issues.description: Add unit test to verify file permissions are set correctly
issues.suggestion: Test that CONFIG_FILE has 0o600 permissions after save_config()

security_concerns.description: FIXED: Removed credential logging that could expose AWS credentials in logs
security_concerns.recommendation: Verify no other locations in codebase log or print credentials

security_concerns.description: FIXED: Added secure file permissions to protect configuration files
security_concerns.recommendation: Consider also checking permissions on existing config files at startup

best_practices.description: Good: Using os.chmod to set restrictive file permissions
Good: Clear comments explaining security fixes

recommendations: Add error handling for os.chmod on Windows (may not support Unix permissions)
Consider adding a security audit function to check existing config file permissions
Add integration test to verify credentials are never logged anywhere in the application
